rationale: The citing paper repeatedly treats Bijmolt et al. (2005) as the main prior empirical generalization on price elasticity and uses it to define the core gap, especially the lack of evidence outside the North-Atlantic region. It also invokes that study when justifying a unified modeling approach for comparability and when interpreting results. In the discussion, the authors directly benchmark their average price elasticity against Bijmolt et al.'s reported mean (-2.62) and analyze reasons for the difference. This is a meaningful framing and comparative role, not a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the line of work in Bijmolt et al. (2005) by generating new cross-national elasticity generalizations in under-studied Indo-Pacific markets and by jointly studying line-length, price, and distribution elasticities. It uses Bijmolt et al. as a primary benchmark for prior scope and magnitude of price elasticity, then contrasts and contextualizes its own estimates.
